verbatim_text: Given this problem, this model throws a ton of computation trying to adapt and try to solve this problem. But at some point, the computation alone is not enough. So whenever it's uncertain, it needs to query an experiment, perhaps a human, perhaps some other system. But it needs to make this verification call quite judiciously. When it knows computation won't solve the problem, we need new data. We need new information. Now, you can incorporate the results from this experiment into this learned algorithm, update its context or its weights. In the case of LLMs, continually improve it over a lifecycle of this improvement. Eventually, we keep running this. We can discover some solution or make some progress towards solving the problem. Now, my future work is divided into multiple components that look at various areas here. And the first trust is around lifelong test time adaptation and continual learning. So here, the goal is to train models not just to solve problems, but to seek informative feedback, especially when verification is hard. Now, currently, like pre-trained models don't really do that.
